Title: Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
Subhead: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two different worlds just 10 kilometers apart
Subhead: On the front line, the small town of Oleshky in the south has been occupied by the Russian military since the invasion began. Residents try to reach Kherson at great personal risk.

Title: 'In a country as polarized as Spain, it is Pedro Sánchez's international stance that garners the most support'
Subhead: The Spanish prime minister's positions on Donald Trump's policies and the situation in the Middle East have drawn fierce criticism from foreign leaders. Yet Sánchez's assertiveness on the world stage stands in sharp contrast to his weakness at home, explains writer Daniel Gascón.

Title: SpaceX postpones highly anticipated Starship launch
Subhead: After several rounds of stopping and starting the countdown clock, the company said engineers would not be able to work through last-minute glitches in time to lift off Thursday. SpaceX is now targeting Friday for another take-off attempt. The trial mission comes amid high stakes for the space company eyeing a blockbuster initial public offering.

Title: Canal+ 'blacklist': Over half of recent French films credit people now threatened with retribution
Subhead: The CEO of Canal+ has threatened to boycott film industry figures who signed an open letter against the company's owner, Vincent Bolloré. Together, the signatories are credited in over 600 films produced in France over the past five years.

How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
Subhead: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

Title: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
Subhead: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

Title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
Subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.
